visual_notes: Chatoyancy (cat's eye effect) is the defining visual feature. The golden-brown silky shimmer moves across the surface as the viewing angle shifts. The parallel fiber orientation is always visible in cross-section.
prompt_fragments: golden silky chatoyant cat's eye effect in amber-brown fibrous silica
predator-eye desert surveillance mineral with shimmering fiber bands
pseudomorph after crocidolite with iron oxide golden shimmer
vibe_notes: Tiger's eye has a quality of focused, watchful warmth — it looks like something is looking back. The chatoyancy gives it movement and life. The amber-gold color range places it in hot, dry, predatory aesthetic territory.
folklore_summary: The cat's eye effect has long been associated with the protective gaze of predatory animals. Tiger's eye has been used as a protective talisman against the evil eye in various traditions.
